label: Grafana Tempo
description: Connect to a Grafana Tempo backend to query and explore distributed traces.

form.title: Connect Grafana Tempo
form.subtitle: Point Anton at your Tempo endpoint to query and explore distributed traces.

methods.label: Tempo URL (no auth)
methods.description: Paste your Tempo HTTP endpoint and connect instantly — ideal for local or unauthenticated Tempo instances.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Tempo with a URL (no auth)

Use this path for local Tempo instances, Docker Compose setups, or any deployment that doesn't require authentication.

### Steps

1. Find the HTTP address of your Tempo instance. The default port is **3200** (e.g. `http://localhost:3200`).
2. If Tempo is running behind a reverse proxy or Grafana Cloud, use the full base URL shown in your Grafana data-source settings under **Data sources → Tempo → URL**.
3. Paste the URL into the **Tempo URL** field below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Anton queries the Tempo HTTP API (`/api/traces`, `/api/search`, etc.) — make sure those paths are reachable from the machine running Anton.
- If your instance requires authentication or multi-tenancy headers, use the **Basic Auth** or **Basic Auth + Org ID** methods instead.

fields.name: tempo_url
fields.label: Tempo URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Tempo HTTP endpoint, including scheme and port.

methods.label: Basic Auth
methods.description: Supply a username and password alongside the Tempo URL — suitable for Tempo instances protected by HTTP basic authentication or Grafana Cloud Tempo.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Tempo with Basic Auth

Use this for Tempo instances (including Grafana Cloud) that require a username and password.

### Steps

1. Find your Tempo base URL. In Grafana Cloud, open **Connections → Data sources → Tempo** and copy the **URL** field (e.g. `https://<stack>.grafana.net/tempo`).
2. For Grafana Cloud, your **username** is the numeric instance ID shown on the same page, and your **password** is a Grafana Cloud API token.
   - Generate a token at: **grafana.com → your org → Security → API Keys → Add API key** with the `MetricsPublisher` or `Viewer` role.
3. For self-hosted Tempo behind a proxy (e.g. nginx with `auth_basic`), use the credentials you configured on that proxy.
4. Paste the URL, username, and password into the fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- The password is stored encrypted locally — never sent to Anton's servers.
- Grafana Cloud tokens can be scoped read-only for safety.

fields.name: tempo_url
fields.label: Tempo URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Tempo HTTP endpoint, including scheme and port.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: For Grafana Cloud, this is the numeric instance ID shown on the data source page.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password / API Token
fields.description: For Grafana Cloud, use a Grafana Cloud API token. For self-hosted, use the proxy password.

methods.label: Basic Auth + Org ID (multi-tenant)
methods.description: For multi-tenant Tempo deployments: add an X-Scope-OrgID header alongside basic auth credentials to route queries to the correct tenant.
methods.how_to:
## Connect to Tempo with Basic Auth and a Tenant Org ID

Tempo's multi-tenant mode (used with Grafana Enterprise or cortex-style deployments) requires an `X-Scope-OrgID` header on every request in addition to any auth credentials.

### Steps

1. Identify your Tempo base URL and basic auth credentials (see **Basic Auth** method above for details).
2. Determine your **Org ID** (tenant ID). This is typically configured in your Tempo or Cortex deployment manifest and shared with you by your platform team. Common values are short strings like `myteam` or `prod`.
3. If running Tempo in single-tenant mode, the org ID is usually `anonymous` — but in that case you likely don't need this method at all.
4. Fill in the URL, username, password, and org ID below, then click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Anton will send `X-Scope-OrgID: <your-value>` on every Tempo API request.
- Mixing up the org ID is a common gotcha: traces stored under tenant A won't appear when querying as tenant B.
- See the [Tempo multi-tenancy docs](https://grafana.com/docs/tempo/latest/operations/tempo_cli/#multi-tenancy) for more context.

fields.name: tempo_url
fields.label: Tempo URL
fields.description: Base URL of your Tempo HTTP endpoint, including scheme and port.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Leave blank if your Tempo endpoint does not require basic authentication.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password / API Token
fields.description: Leave blank if your Tempo endpoint does not require basic authentication.

fields.name: org_id
fields.label: X-Scope-OrgID
fields.description: Tenant identifier sent as the X-Scope-OrgID header. Use 'anonymous' for single-tenant deployments that still require the header.
